story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that Iran and the US have reached a tentative agreement, pending approval from former President Trump. Analysts warn that escalating conflict in West Asia poses a stagflationary risk to the global economy, while India’s crude processing declined by 8.9% in April due to shifting Middle East supply dynamics. Additionally, concerns persist over potential oil shortages threatening global energy security.

story_clusters.summary: India and South Korea have agreed to address India's widening trade deficit during the 12th review of their Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA). Discussions also included expanding talks on digital trade and supply chains.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) imposed monetary penalties on three co-operative banks—Shree Laxmi Co-operative Bank Ltd. (Pune), The Jalore Central Co-operative Bank Limited (Rajasthan), and The Lunawada People’s Co-operative Bank Ltd. (Gujarat)—for regulatory violations. Separately, a CNBC report noted that India’s bank non-performing assets (NPAs) reached a multi-decadal low in FY26, according to an RBI report.

perspectives.unique_angle: RBI should avoid aggressive rate hikes as high crude threatens growth, says Axis AMC’s Devang Shah Devang Shah, Head of Fixed Income at Axis AMC believes sharply tighter financial conditions could hur

story_clusters.summary: Former RBI Governor Duvvuri Subbarao has argued that the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) should allow the rupee to depreciate and rely on liquidity tools like the Variable Rate Repo (VRR) rather than raising interest rates to control inflation. The RBI has announced plans to conduct a three-day VRR auction under the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) on May 29, 2026. Axis AMC’s Devang Shah has also cautioned against aggressive rate hikes, citing risks to growth from high crude prices.

The move aligns with global trends, as several countries have adopted polymer notes for their longevity and security features.

articles.title: SC Upholds Bihar Special Intensive Revision (SIR) Power of ECI
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the Election Commission of India's (ECI) constitutional power to conduct a Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Bihar. The Court ruled that the ECI's authority under Article 324 and Section 21(3) of the Representation of the People Act remains valid unless it violates explicit statutory provisions. This decision reinforces the ECI's regulatory role in maintaining accurate electoral rolls.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India, in a Constitution Bench ruling, held that courts can modify arbitral awards in limited cases, such as correcting manifest errors or severable portions. The judgment recognized an implied power under Section 34 of the Arbitration Act. This decision clarifies judicial oversight in arbitration while leaving some ambiguities unresolved.

articles.title: S.33(1)(a) Arbitration Act Only To Correct Clerical Errors In Award; Can't Be Invoked To Change Nature Of Interest : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Section 33(1)(a) of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, can only correct clerical errors in an arbitral award and cannot be used to change the nature of interest awarded (e.g., from simple to compound interest). The bench of Justice PS Narasimha and Justice Alok Aradhe allowed appeals filed by the Gujarat Water Supply and Sewerage Board, clarifying the limited scope of error correction under the law.
articles.raw_summary: Holding that substance of an arbitral award cannot be altered under the guise of correcting errors, the Supreme Court has ruled that changing the nature of interest awarded from simple interest to compound interest amounts to a substantive modification that falls outside the limited scope of Section 33(1)(a) of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. A bench of Justice PS Narasimha and Justice Alok Aradhe passed the ruling while allowing appeals filed by the Gujarat Water Supply and Sewerage

articles.title: Decree Without Jurisdiction and Reasons Is Inexecutable: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a court decree passed without addressing jurisdiction or providing judicial reasoning is unenforceable. The judgment emphasizes that such decrees lack legal validity and cannot be executed. This decision impacts how lower courts handle cases and reinforces procedural fairness in judicial proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a decree passed without deciding maintainability and jurisdiction issues cannot be executed. It held that absence of judicial reasoning rendered the decree void and unenforceable. The post Decree Without Jurisdiction and Reasons Is Inexecutable: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Limitation Is Not a Jurisdiction Issue Under Section 9A CPC: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that limitation periods cannot be treated as a preliminary issue under Section 9A of the Code of Civil Procedure (CPC). The court clarified that Section 9A only applies to defects related to a court's competence to hear a suit, not to limitation issues. This ruling impacts how courts handle suits where limitation is a point of contention. The decision was delivered by the Supreme Court.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that limitation cannot be treated as a preliminary issue under Section 9A of the CPC because it does not affect the courts jurisdiction to entertain a suit. The ruling clarified that Section 9A applies only to defects relating to the courts competence to receive the suit. The post Limitation Is Not a Jurisdiction Issue Under Section 9A CPC: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Being in electoral rolls no lifelong guarantee—what SC said on 1995 judgment at heart of SIR verdict
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the Election Commission of India's (ECI) Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process, clarifying that being on electoral rolls does not guarantee lifelong inclusion. The court ruled that the presumption of validity in a 1995 judgment does not restrict the ECI's power to conduct SIR. The decision reinforces the ECI's authority to maintain accurate electoral rolls, ensuring fair elections in India.
articles.raw_summary: Upholding poll panel’s Special Intensive Revision, SC explains ‘context’ of 1995 decision. Presumption of validity is no ‘blanket embargo’ on ECI power to conduct SIR, court rules.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld Section 63(4) of the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, ruling that requiring the disclosure of a hash value and expert certification ensures the authenticity and integrity of electronic records. articles.title: FIR Against an Advocate for Filing a GST Appeal Quashed: A Defining Constitutional Moment for the Independence of the Bar
articles.summary: The court quashed an FIR filed against an advocate for filing a GST appeal, ruling that prosecuting a lawyer for professional legal work misuses criminal law and threatens the justice system. The court emphasized that disagreement with legal interpretation cannot be treated as criminal conspiracy. This decision is seen as a defining moment for the independence of the legal profession in India.

articles.title: Legal Digest | Why is gas supply through an inter-state pipeline not subject to state tax
articles.summary: A recent Supreme Court judgment reaffirms that gas supply through inter-state pipelines is exempt from state sales tax (21%) and subject to central sales tax (4%) when the movement is tied to a sale contract. Chartered Accountant S Murlidharan explains this long-standing CST jurisprudence in a Legal Digest column for CNBC TV18, citing the court's ruling as a binding precedent for tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: It has been a time-honored principle of CST jurisprudence that if an inter-state movement of goods is caused directly or indirectly by a contract of sale, it is deemed to be an inter-state sale and the CST rate of 4% will apply and not the state sales tax rate of 21%, notes Chartered Accountant S Murlidharan in this week's Legal Digest referring to a recent Supreme Court judgement.

A record 2.87 trillion rupees will be transferred to the federal government.

articles.summary: State-run Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals (GSFC) announced that the Indian government will cover higher raw material costs through subsidies to protect profit margins on essential fertilizers like Urea and Diammonium Phosphate (DAP). The move comes amid rising input costs due to geopolitical conflicts in West Asia. The subsidy aims to stabilize fertilizer prices and ensure supply for Indian farmers. GSFC is a key player in India's fertilizer sector.
articles.raw_summary: The state-run company said government subsidies will absorb higher input costs and protect margins in key products such as Urea and Diammonium Phosphate (DAP), triggered by geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

articles.title: Permission From Magistrate Necessary For Police To Conduct Further Investigation: Supreme Court Reiterates
articles.summary: The Supreme Court reiterated that police must obtain express permission from a magistrate before conducting further investigation after filing a closure report. A bench of Justices Sanjay Karol and Nongmeikapam Kotiswar Singh observed that while the statute does not explicitly require this permission, judicial precedent has established it as a necessary step. The ruling reinforces legal safeguards in criminal investigations and impacts police procedural compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has reiterated that in the absence of an express permission from the magistrate, a further investigation cannot be proceeded with by the police after filing of a closure report. “Even though the statute does not require express permission, the law as it has developed, has made abundantly clear that seeking of permission from the concerned Magistrate has evolved into a requirement.” , observed a bench of Justice Sanjay Karol and Justice Nongmeikapam Kotiswar Singh. articles.summary: China has been blocked from importing advanced chip-making machines since 2019, limiting its ability to produce cutting-edge semiconductors. Huawei is now focusing on alternative methods to maintain competitiveness amid these restrictions. The situation highlights global semiconductor supply chain challenges and geopolitical tensions over technology access.

articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

articles.title: GST AAR Rejects Hotel ITC Plea as Enforcement Proceedings Were Already Pending
articles.summary: The West Bengal GST Advance Ruling Authority (AAR) rejected a hotel's plea for Input Tax Credit (ITC) on construction costs after finding that enforcement proceedings on the same issue were already underway. The authority cited Section 98(2) of the GST law, which bars admission of applications where identical questions are pending before tax authorities. This ruling impacts how businesses can claim ITC and reinforces the government's stance on pending tax disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The West Bengal AAR rejected an advance ruling application on hotel construction ITC after finding that GST enforcement proceedings on the same issue had already begun. articles.title: Twisha Sharma Case: Six Ante-Mortem Injuries, Forced Abortion Allegations, Non-Cooperation — Why MP HC Quashed Mother-in-Law’s Anticipatory Bail
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court rejected anticipatory bail for a mother-in-law in the Twisha Sharma case after noting six ante-mortem injuries on the deceased’s body and allegations of forced abortion. The court highlighted non-cooperation and the circumstances of the death, which was ruled a suicide by hanging but with unexplained injuries. The ruling underscores legal scrutiny in cases involving vulnerable groups and potential systemic gaps in addressing such incidents.
articles.raw_summary: The Court noted that as per the post-mortem report, the death was due to ante-mortem hanging by ligature, but 6 other ante-mortem injuries were found on the body of the deceased, which were not caused by taking the body out of the ligature or carrying it out to the hospital. articles.title: SC puts cheque bounce-IBC issue before larger bench
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has referred a key legal issue to a larger bench regarding cheque bounce cases under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The question is whether the IBC moratorium suspends or completely extinguishes penal consequences for dishonored cheques issued by insolvent entities. The larger bench's decision will clarify the interplay between IBC provisions and criminal liability under the Negotiable Instruments Act.

PFAS, known as 'forever chemicals' due to their persistence in the environment, have been linked to long-term ecological and health concerns. This case underscores growing global scrutiny of industrial chemicals and their regulation.

articles.title: Laundry Soap Not Eligible for 5% GST as It Is Distinct from Toilet Soap
articles.summary: The West Bengal Authority for Advance Ruling (AAR) ruled that laundry soap and toilet soap are distinct products under GST. It held that laundry soap bars weighing less than 500 grams are taxable at 18% instead of the 5% rate applicable to toilet soap. This decision clarifies GST classification for soap products and impacts tax liability for manufacturers and sellers.

The ruling addresses prosecutions arising from matrimonial disputes and clarifies legal standards for such cases. This decision impacts how domestic violence and dowry-related cases are handled in Indian courts. The court emphasized the need for concrete evidence to avoid frivolous prosecutions.
articles.raw_summary: Allegations against each accused 'must be specific, distinct and supported by prima facie material' in prosecutions arising out of matrimonial disputes, it asserted.

articles.title: India mulls export of indigenous Vande Bharat trains, new platform coming up
articles.summary: India is eyeing exports of the standard gauge version of its indigenously-developed semi-high-speed Vande Bharat Express trains amid interest from several nations. These exports will help establish India's presence in the lucrative global market for high-tech urban mobility products.

articles.title: Piyush Goyal wraps up Canada visit focusing on India-Canada CEPA talks, $50 billion trade goal
articles.summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal concluded a three-day visit to Canada. The trip focused on boosting trade and investment between India and Canada. Discussions also centered on the ongoing Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement negotiations. The visit reinforced strong economic ties and highlighted India as a prime investment destination. Engagements included meetings with government leaders, business councils, and the Indian diaspora.

Engagements included meetings with government leaders, business councils, and the Indian diaspora.

articles.title: Tax ruling can set off a game of thorns for gaming company directors
articles.summary: India's money gaming firms are worried about directors facing tax demands. A Supreme Court ruling upheld a 28% GST on bets. This could mean personal liability for company board members if taxes remain unpaid. Companies are hoping for relief from the ruling's details. The government's amnesty scheme might offer a way out for earlier periods.

The government's amnesty scheme might offer a way out for earlier periods.

articles.title: Dera chief Ram Rahim walks out of jail on 30-day parole
articles.summary: Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh walked out of jail on a 30-day parole. Sikh organizations, including the Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee, have previously opposed the repeated granting of parole and furlough to Ram Rahim, citing concerns over the legal process. The parole decision has sparked criticism over the application of prison policies.
articles.raw_summary: Sikh organisations, including the Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee, have previously criticised the repeated grant of parole and furlough to Ram Rahim.

articles.title: Singapore court orders jail term for Byju Raveendran for contempt of court
articles.summary: A Singapore court has sentenced Byju Raveendran, founder of BYJU’S, to six months in jail for contempt of court. The court found he disobeyed multiple orders related to his assets and directed him to surrender to authorities and pay costs of S$90,000. This ruling highlights issues of corporate accountability and legal compliance in cross-border contexts.
articles.raw_summary: The Singapore court has sentenced Byju Raveendran, the founder of BYJU’S, to six months in jail. The court found that Raveendran had disobeyed multiple orders related to his assets, and directed him to surrender to authorities and pay costs of S$90,000.

articles.title: The new fault line in Manipur—inside growing Naga-Kuki conflict & why it’s worrying security agencies
articles.summary: Manipur is experiencing escalating clashes between Naga and Kuki tribal groups, adding to existing Meitei-Kuki violence. The conflict is raising fresh security concerns for agencies and could have broader implications for tribal rights and governance in the state. The violence reflects deep-seated ethnic tensions that may require policy attention or intervention from authorities.
articles.raw_summary: After three years of Meitei-Kuki violence, rising clashes between Nagas and Kukis are reshaping Manipur’s conflict and triggering fresh security concerns.

articles.title: Bharti Airtel defends 'Priority Postpaid' service before DoT panel, denies net neutrality violations
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel defended its 'Priority Postpaid' service before a Department of Telecommunications (DoT) panel, denying allegations of net neutrality violations. The company argued that restricting mainstream 5G features could hinder the potential of future 6G technologies in India. The DoT panel is reviewing the service's compliance with telecom regulations.
articles.raw_summary: Bharti Airtel said not allowing the use of mainstream features of 5G to offer services will jeopardise the potential of 6G in the country.

articles.title: Coach shortage, delay in rearrangement affect train punctuality, standards: Railway Board panel

The committee's recommendations aim to guide the development of large-scale coaching depots. The report is expected to inform future policy decisions for railway infrastructure improvements.
articles.raw_summary: The committee’s recommendation, compiled as a ’Report on Requirements of facilities for Mega Coaching Depots’ aimed to serve as a guiding document for the development of such depots.

articles.title: In Pakistan too, ethnic Bengalis face challenges with citizenship papers
articles.summary: In Pakistan, many ethnic Bengalis have been incorrectly classified as foreigners, denying them access to education, healthcare, employment, and marriage opportunities. This issue highlights broader challenges faced by minority groups in securing citizenship rights. While the article focuses on Pakistan, it may influence discussions on similar rights violations in India, particularly concerning refugee and minority policies.
articles.raw_summary: Many Pakistani Bengalis have mistakenly been classified as foreigners. This hurts their prospects for education, healthcare access, employment and marriage.

articles.title: Hostile Witness Testimony Can Support Acquittal Too, Not Merely Conviction: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that testimony from a hostile witness can support an accused person's acquittal if it creates serious doubts about the prosecution's evidence. This clarification comes in a case where the witness turned hostile during trial. The ruling underscores that hostile witness testimony is not solely useful for convictions but can also aid in acquittals.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court clarifies that hostile witness testimony can be used to acquit an accused if it creates a serious doubt about the prosecution's evidence.

articles.title: Absence of Accused’s Name in Inquest Report Not Proof of Innocence: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the absence of an accused person's name in an inquest report does not prove their innocence. The Court clarified that inquest reports only determine the apparent cause of death and cannot weaken a prosecution case on their own. This judgment reinforces the legal principle that such reports are not conclusive evidence in criminal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: Inquest reports only ascertain the apparent cause of death; absence of the accused’s name cannot by itself weaken the prosecution, says the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Predator-prey relationships and habitat quality shape a tiger reserve
articles.summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. After reintroduction, a study found that prey availability and habitat quality are crucial for tiger population recovery. The findings underscore the need for balanced conservation strategies beyond just reintroducing carnivores. The research was published by Mongabay India.
articles.raw_summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. They were reintroduced a few years later and have since re-established populations. While carnivore reintroduction has been considered as an important strategy to restore ecological balance, a recent study highlights that prey abundance and habitat quality also play an essential role. articles.title: Game of skills is gambling if players put money on ‘uncertain outcomes’: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that even games requiring skill can be classified as gambling under India's GST law if players wager money on uncertain outcomes. The court also clarified that online gambling proceeds are taxable. This decision has significant implications for the regulation and taxation of skill-based games in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court said even if a game requires skill, it becomes gambling under the GST law when players risk their money on uncertain outcomes and laid out how online gambling money is taxable. The post Game of skills is gambling if players put money on ‘uncertain outcomes’: SC appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

articles.title: Byju Raveendran sentenced to 6 months in jail by Singapore court for contempt
articles.summary: Singapore’s court has sentenced Byju Raveendran, founder of the failed Indian edtech firm Byju’s, to 6 months in jail for contempt. The court ordered him to surrender, pay a $70,500 fine, and submit documents proving legal ownership of a corporate entity linked to a related company. The ruling highlights legal accountability for corporate governance failures in a high-profile Indian startup.
articles.raw_summary: The founder of failed tech firm Byju's has been instructed to surrender, pay $70,500   provide documents proving legal ownership of a corporate entity that held shares in a related company.

articles.title: In HC, Asaram claimed ‘media propaganda’ vitiated trial in rape case. How court deflated his argument
articles.summary: In 2018, Asaram was convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment for raping a minor who came to him for 'spiritual healing'. The High Court has now upheld both the conviction and the sentence. Asaram had argued that 'media propaganda' had vitiated the trial, but the court rejected this claim. The case highlights issues of child protection and the legal process in sexual violence cases.
articles.raw_summary: In 2018, Asaram was convicted and given life term for raping a minor when she came to him for ‘spiritual healing’, The HC has upheld the conviction and sentence.

articles.title: Can an adult be denied employment for offence committed as juvenile? Allahabad HC answers
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled on whether an adult can be denied employment due to an offence committed as a juvenile. The case involved a candidate who cleared the MTS exam under the Employees' State Insurance Corporation but had their offer letter withheld. The court's decision addresses employment rights and juvenile justice, setting a legal precedent for similar cases.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling came in a case related to a candidate who cleared MTS exam under Employees' State Insurance Corporation, but whose offer letter was withheld.

articles.title: When ‘love’ turns sour—Muslim bindi seller’s 18-yr legal battle against false rape, kidnapping charge
articles.summary: Mohammad Quasim, a Muslim bindi seller, spent 18 years fighting false charges of rape and kidnapping after marrying outside his community in 2004. The case highlights the prolonged legal struggles faced by individuals accused of crimes they did not commit, raising concerns about justice and civil liberties in India.
articles.raw_summary: Bindi-seller Mohammad Quasim went to jail, and then fought a legal battle for 18 years to clear his name after marrying outside community for love back in 2004.

articles.title: Embraer takes on Lockheed, says its KC-390 is 30% cheaper, better suited for IAF than C-130J
articles.summary: Embraer has claimed its KC-390 military transport aircraft is 30% cheaper and better suited for the Indian Air Force (IAF) than Lockheed Martin’s C-130J Hercules. The KC-390 can carry up to 26 tonnes of payload, compared to the C-130J’s 19 tonnes, while Airbus’s A400M can lift 37 tonnes. The comparison highlights potential procurement implications for India’s defence acquisitions.
articles.raw_summary: Lockheed Martin’s C-130J Hercules at lower end of requirement with payload of about 19 tonnes, KC-390 near upper limit with 26 tonnes, while Airbus A 400M can lift 37 tonnes.

articles.title: China, Pakistan rake up Kashmir in joint statement after Sharif visit; term it ‘left over from history’
articles.summary: China and Pakistan issued a joint statement after Pakistan's Prime Minister Sharif's visit, referring to Kashmir as a 'left over from history' and reiterating that the dispute should be resolved peacefully under UNSC resolutions and bilateral agreements. The statement was shared by Pakistan with China, which reaffirmed its position on the matter. This development is relevant to India due to its territorial and strategic implications.
articles.raw_summary: According to statement, Pakistan briefed China on 'latest developments', China reiterated position that dispute should be peacefully resolved in accordance with UNSC resolutions, bilateral agreements.

articles.title: Quad condemns Pahalgam attack, calls for sustained action against terror proxies
articles.summary: The Quad, a diplomatic alliance including India, condemned the Pahalgam attack in April 2025 and the Bondi Beach attack in Australia in December 2024, denouncing terrorism in all its forms. The group called for sustained action against terror proxies. The statement reflects India's foreign policy position on counter-terrorism and international cooperation.
articles.raw_summary: In joint statement, 4 members denounced ‘terrorism in all its forms   manifestations’, specifically referencing Pahalgam attack in April 2025   Bondi Beach attack in Australia in December last year.

articles.title: ‘Make reels, back OSM, don't panic’: Schools get script from CBSE amid backlash
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) issued a document titled 'Material for Principals' with scripts for school heads to read out amid public backlash. The document includes instructions to describe the board as 'highly proactive' and suggests creating content for social media platforms like Instagram Reels. This action reflects CBSE's efforts to manage communication and public perception regarding its policies or decisions.
articles.raw_summary: A document titled “Material for Principals” included scripts of what to read out. At one point, it urged principals to describe the board as “highly proactive".

articles.title: ET Graphics | Base to basket: India's IIP gets a major makeover
articles.summary: A new Index of Industrial Production launches on June 1. The base year shifts to 2022-23. Coverage expands to include minor minerals, rare earths, and waste management. One hundred twenty new items are added, such as CCTV cameras and vaccines. Sixty-four items are removed. This update provides a more comprehensive view of industrial activity.

articles.title: PIL seeks CBI probe into Cockroach Janta Party activities, alleges misuse of court remarks
articles.summary: A public interest litigation (PIL) has been filed in the Supreme Court requesting a CBI investigation into the activities of the 'Cockroach Janta Party'. The plea alleges misuse of the apex court's remarks for commercial exploitation and highlights concerns over fake advocates with fraudulent degrees. It also warns against turning constitutional proceedings into publicity stunts for digital campaigns.
articles.raw_summary: A public interest litigation filed in the Supreme Court has sought a CBI probe into the activities of the self-styled “Cockroach Janta Party”, alleging misuse and commercial exploitation of remarks made by the apex court. The plea also raises concerns over fake advocates with alleged fraudulent degrees and warns against the commodification of constitutional proceedings for publicity and digital campaigns.

articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar is set to take over as the state's top leader following the resignation of Chief Minister Siddaramaiah. The transition is part of a party-led leadership change in the state government. This shift in administration may impact governance and policy priorities in Karnataka.

articles.title: No fuel shortage, says IOC amid panic buying at pumps
articles.summary: Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) stated that only a very small number of its over 42,000 fuel stations faced supply disruptions amid panic buying at pumps. The majority of outlets had normal and adequate stocks and supplies. The company attributed the disruptions to localized issues rather than a broader fuel shortage.
articles.raw_summary: Only a "very small number" of outlets in its network of more than 42,000 fuel stations had witnessed supply disruptions, while stocks and supplies at the majority of pumps remained normal and adequate, the company said in a statement.

articles.title: Video of utensils being washed in train toilet prompts FSSAI notice to IRCTC
articles.summary: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) issued a notice to the Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC) after a viral video allegedly showed catering staff washing utensils inside a train toilet on the LTT-ERS Duronto Express. This raises serious food safety concerns as the practice violates hygiene standards. FSSAI has demanded an explanation from IRCTC within 15 days.
articles.raw_summary: FSSAI has issued a notice to IRCTC after a viral video allegedly showed catering staff washing utensils inside a train toilet on the LTT-ERS Duronto Express, raising food safety concerns.

articles.title: No girl should give up education over lack of toilets or sanitary pads: SC tells Centre
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan) directed the central government to ensure that girls are not compelled to leave school because of inadequate sanitation facilities like toilets or sanitary pads. The bench emphasized that schools must provide safe and hygienic conditions to prevent girls from being pushed into domestic responsibilities. This ruling addresses a critical barrier to girls' education and highlights the state's obligation to uphold their right to schooling.
articles.raw_summary: A Bench of Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan observed that girls should not be pushed into domestic responsibilities or forced to stay at home simply because schools fail to provide safe sanitation facilities.

articles.title: Ajit Doval meets Russian counterpart in Moscow, reviews security and energy ties
articles.summary: India's National Security Advisor Ajit Doval met with his Russian counterpart in Moscow to review bilateral security and energy cooperation. Doval emphasized the need for responsible nations to take clear positions against sponsors of terrorism. The meeting highlights ongoing diplomatic engagement between the two countries on strategic issues.
articles.raw_summary: Ajit Doval said responsible nations must make clear choices on whether they support sponsors of terrorism or counter them with decisive action.

articles.title: Telangana firm Coempt Edutech in focus amid CBSE-OSM row
articles.summary: A controversy surrounding the CBSE's Online School Management (OSM) system has brought attention to Coempt Edutech, a Telangana-based Edutech firm. Congress MP Rahul Gandhi recently criticized the CBSE over complaints related to OSM, raising questions about the regulation and oversight of such educational technology platforms in India. The issue highlights concerns about data privacy, accountability, and the role of private firms in public education systems.
articles.raw_summary: Complains around OSM have brought in focus a Telangana firm, Coempt Edutech, which Congress MP Rahul Gandhi cited in his recent criticism of the CBSE.

articles.title: Indore ice cream factory shut down over ‘extremely unsanitary conditions’

The action was taken following orders from Chief Minister Mohan Yadav to crack down on food adulteration. The closure highlights enforcement of food safety regulations under government policy.
articles.raw_summary: The Food Safety Department raided Siddhivinayak Ice Cream Factory in Indore, after receiving orders from Chief Minister Mohan Yadav to take strict action against food adulteration.

articles.title: 4 lion cubs die in Gujarat's Gir forest region, 17 isolated amid viral infection scare

The incident was reported by Principal Secretary, Forest and Environment, Vinod Rao, to the chief minister during a meeting in Gandhinagar. This raises concerns about wildlife health and conservation policies in India's protected areas.

articles.summary: India is updating its Index of Industrial Production. The new series will use 2022-23 as the base year, replacing the old 2011-12 base. articles.title: India may cheer an Iran deal, but peace in Hormuz won’t refill its emptying economic tank
articles.summary: The economic horizon of India is clouded by a dual predicament characterized by a lack of financial resources and innovative ideas. Dwindling foreign investments coupled with a slow pace of domestic creativity stymie progress, particularly affecting employment rates for the youth.

Anil Trigunayat.

The US also intercepted drones targeting a commercial ship and struck a launching unit. The impasse in talks and military actions raises concerns about regional stability and potential disruptions to global oil supplies.

articles.title: Aluminium rises to a four-year high amid fears of output cut by China as war takes a toll
articles.summary: Aluminium prices reached a four-year high due to Middle East supply disruptions and fears of reduced production in China, which is a major global supplier. Copper prices, meanwhile, eased after a recent rally driven by AI-related infrastructure demand. The situation may influence India's trade and energy policies, particularly regarding metal imports and supply chain resilience.

articles.summary: A bus traveling on the Lucknow-Agra Expressway in Uttar Pradesh's Unnao district overturned after hitting a divider, killing six people. Eyewitnesses claimed the bus was speeding at the time of the incident. The vehicle became suspended on the roadside railing following the crash. The accident highlights concerns about road safety and infrastructure on state highways.
articles.raw_summary: Eyewitnesses said the bus was speeding when it suddenly hit the divider and overturned. Following the impact, the vehicle got suspended on the roadside railing.

articles.summary: Protesters in Manipur's capital Imphal demanded the release of six civilians and the removal of Deputy Chief Minister Nemcha Kipgen from the state council of ministers. The protesters alleged that her husband heads a militant outfit, Kuki National Front (Presidential), and was responsible for the abduction of the civilians. The demands reflect concerns over governance and civil liberties in the region.

articles.title: Supreme Court refuses to hear plea challenging Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann’s remarks on stray dog elimination
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declined to hear a petition challenging Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann’s announcement of a campaign to eliminate stray dogs. Mann had earlier thanked the Supreme Court for permitting such action in a social media post. The plea was filed in response to the state government’s proposed measures, raising questions about animal welfare and public health policies.
articles.raw_summary: The plea was filed after Bhagwant Mann announced on social media that Punjab would launch a campaign against stray dogs, thanking the Supreme Court for permitting such action.

articles.title: IGL raises CNG prices again; rates up ₹4/kg in May
articles.summary: Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL) increased CNG prices by ₹4/kg in May, while fuel retailers raised petrol and diesel prices for the third time this month, cumulatively adding nearly ₹5 per litre. The hikes are attributed to rising input costs and elevated global energy prices. These developments could prompt government or regulatory responses, such as policy measures to address fuel affordability or inflation.
articles.raw_summary: Earlier today, fuel retailers also raised petrol and diesel prices for the third time this month, taking the cumulative increase in auto fuel prices to nearly ₹5 per litre. The hikes come amid rising input costs and elevated global energy prices.

articles.title: Anthropic's Olah says AI must be guided from outside Big Tech
articles.summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Olah stated that AI companies face commercial, geopolitical, and personal pressures that may conflict with broader societal interests. He emphasized the need for external guidance to ensure AI development aligns with public good. The remarks underscore debates around AI regulation and oversight, particularly in India where tech governance is a growing policy concern.
articles.raw_summary: Anthropic co-founder said that companies like his faced strong commercial, geopolitical and ​personal pressures that can be at odds with the broader interests of society.

articles.title: Huawei plans to build 1.4nm chips without the machine that powers TSMC, Samsung, and Intel
articles.summary: Huawei has announced plans to produce 1.4nm semiconductor chips by 2031 using its new LogicFolding technology, aiming to reduce its five-year gap with leading chipmakers like TSMC. The company claims it can achieve this without relying on ASML's EUV lithography machines, which are currently banned for Huawei. This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's push for domestic chip manufacturing under initiatives like the Semiconductor Mission.
articles.raw_summary: Huawei says its new LogicFolding tech can produce 1.4nm chips by 2031, narrowing a five-year gap with TSMC — without relying on ASML's banned EUV lithography machines.
